sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 11.90 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.11)

statement.latex: Let${ V}$be a variety of groups and let$k$be an infinite cardinal number. A~group$G { V}$of rank$k$is called almost free in${ V$\/} if each of its subgroups of rank less than$k$is contained in a subgroup of$G$which is free in${ V}$. For what~$k$do there exist almost free but not free in${ B}$groups of rank$k$?

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
